channel.title: OOIR Trends: Clinical Medicine (Nutrition & Dietetics)
channel.description: Aktuelle Paper-Trends im Bereich Clinical Medicine (Nutrition & Dietetics) von OOIR (mit Titel und Metadaten von Crossref)

item.title: Egg Intake and the Incidence of Alzheimer’s Disease in the Adventist Health Study-2 Cohort Linked with Medicare Data
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Nutrition & Dietetics Score: 966 Autoren: Jisoo Oh, Keiji Oda, Gabriela Chiriac, Gary E Fraser, Rawiwan Sirirat, Joan Sabaté Journal: The Journal of Nutrition Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.tjnut.2026.101541 ISSN: 0022-3166 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-05-16

item.title: The effect of increasing availability of vegetarian meals on their sales in worksite cafeterias: a stepped-wedge cluster randomised controlled trial
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Nutrition & Dietetics Score: 289 Autoren: Elisa Becker, Emma E Garnett, Peter Scarborough, Steven Cummins, Bea Savory, Oliver Huse, Claire Thompson, Jessica Brock, Michael Clark, Jessica Renzella, Martin White, Rachel Pechey Journal: International Journal of Behavioral Nutrition and Physical Activity Veröffentlicht: 2026-04-15 DOI: 10.1186/s12966-026-01889-x ISSN: 1479-5868 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-05-16

item.title: GLP-1 agonists and changes in body mass and composition in adults with overweight or obesity with or without type 2 diabetes mellitus: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Nutrition & Dietetics Score: 69 Autoren: Nadia Sawicka-Gutaj, Dawid Gruszczyński, Kacper Nijakowski, Aleksandra Derwich-Rudowicz, Michal Krenz, Anita Kumar, Pei-Yin Lu, Marek Ruchała Journal: International Journal of Obesity Veröffentlicht: 2026-04-25 DOI: 10.1038/s41366-026-02088-1 ISSN: 0307-0565 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-05-16

item.title: Prevalence of sustainability claims on packaged food
item.description:
Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Nutrition & Dietetics Score: 46 Autoren: Mariel Keaney, Eden M. Barrett, Mike Rayner, Simone Pettigrew, Alexandra Jones Journal: Public Health Nutrition Veröffentlicht: 2026-04-28 Abstract: Abstract
Objective: Global food systems have a key influence on both health and sustainability. Dietary shifts that promote health and sustainability are regarded as a critical synergistic pathway for advancing human and planetary health. In response, consumer interest in the sustainability of food systems has prompted the use of claims highlighting positive sustainability attributes displayed on-pack. This study assessed the prevalence and types of sustainability claims displayed on packaged food in Australia.
Design: Claims data on packaged food were collected at five supermarkets in Sydney in 2024. We determined the prevalence of sustainability claims overall, by claim category and format (text or logo).
Setting: Australia
Participants: None
Results: Over a third of products displayed at least one sustainability claim, the most prevalent being natural and vegan. Text claims were far more prevalent than logos (84% v 16% of total claims identified on pack, respectively).
Conclusions: Frequent use of broad and unspecific sustainability claims raises challenges for substantiation, and there is a risk that consumers are being misled. Our findings support the need for stronger regulation, including criteria for terms used in claims, to improve consumer trust and comparability across products.
DOI: 10.1017/s1368980026102511 ISSN: 1368-9800 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-05-16

item.title: Eating while distracted: a systematic review and meta-analysis of the effect of distraction on concurrent and later energy intake in adults
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Nutrition & Dietetics Score: 29 Autoren: Thomas Gough, Traci Mann, Khaleda Ahmadyar, Isla Finlay, Andrew Jones, Katy Tapper, Eric Robinson Journal: The American Journal of Clinical Nutrition Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.ajcnut.2026.101315 ISSN: 0002-9165 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-05-16
